name: Checkly
what_it_does: Checkly is a programmable synthetic monitoring platform with a REST API for managing API checks, browser-based E2E checks (Playwright), alert channels, and check results across global locations.
use_cases: Create and update API endpoint health checks programmatically as part of a CI/CD pipeline deployment workflow
Retrieve recent check run results and failure logs to diagnose production incidents from an AI ops agent
Manage alert channel configurations (Slack, PagerDuty, email) to wire monitoring into incident response workflows
Trigger manual check executions on-demand to verify a deployment succeeded before marking it complete
List all failing checks across environments to build a real-time service health summary for a status dashboard
not_for: Unit or integration testing within CI pipelines — Checkly runs synthetic checks from external locations, not inside your test runner
Log aggregation or full APM — Checkly measures availability and correctness, not traces or deep performance profiling
Monitoring non-HTTP services such as databases, message queues, or internal gRPC endpoints not exposed publicly
best_when: You need programmable synthetic monitoring defined as code (Checkly CLI / Monitoring as Code) and want to assert on API correctness and browser user flows from global locations.
avoid_when: You need passive APM instrumentation, real-user monitoring, or monitoring of internal private services not reachable from Checkly's global runner network.

auth.notes: API key passed as Bearer token in Authorization header; keys are account-scoped with no per-resource granularity currently.

pricing.free_tier_limits: 50,000 check runs/month, limited browser check minutes, 1 user
pricing.paid_tiers: Team plan from ~$80/month with higher check run volumes, team collaboration, and private locations
pricing.notes: Pricing scales by check run volume and browser check minutes; Enterprise tier available for private runners and SLAs.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: API returns structured JSON errors with message and validation field details; HTTP status codes are used correctly and consistently.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: GET and DELETE are idempotent; POST check creation is not — retrying without idempotency key creates duplicate checks.
known_agent_gotchas: Check IDs are UUIDs; agents must list checks first rather than constructing IDs — there is no stable slug-based addressing
Triggering a check run via the API returns a run ID but results are asynchronous — agents must poll the results endpoint with backoff
Rate limit of 100 req/min is enforced; agents running bulk check updates (e.g., after a deploy) can easily hit this limit
Webhook signatures use HMAC-SHA256 but verification docs are sparse — agents consuming webhooks should implement signature validation
The Monitoring as Code (CLI) approach and REST API are separate paradigms; checks created via CLI may not be fully editable via REST API without state conflicts

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2 Type II certified; API keys lack fine-grained scopes which is the main auth weakness; webhook HMAC verification is available but not well documented.
